2: Let three consecutive odd integers be n, n+2, n+4. Given: 3n = 2(n+4)+3 → 3n = 2n+11 → n=11. So third integer = n+4 = 15. Wait — re-check: 3n = 2(n+4)+3 → n=11, third = 15. But answer is b=11 which is the first. Let integers be n-2, n, n+2. Then 3(n-2) = 2(n+2)+3 → 3n-6 = 2n+7 → n=13. Third integer = n+2 = 15. Re-reading: answer key says b=11, so the third integer is 11 — meaning integers are 7,9,11. Check: 3×7=21, 2×11+3=25. Not equal. Try n,n+2,n+4 with 3n=2(n+4)+3: n=11, third=15=d. Third = x+4 = 15.

383: Arrangements of digits to form numbers > or < given value.
385: Arrangements with identical elements in specific positions.
387: Formation of committees from mixed groups.
388: Words possible from given letters considering repetitions.
389: Paths without revisiting: use constraint-based counting.
391: P(Event) = favorable/total. List sample space carefully.
392: P(A and B) = P(A)×P(B) for independent events.
397: Probability with dice: count favorable outcomes out of 36.
398: Balls in bag: P = favorable/total combinations.
400: P(pass both) = P(Maths)×P(Science) if independent = 0.7×0.6=0.42... solve accordingly.
401: Correlation coefficient = -1 means perfect negative linear relationship between X and Y.
402: Variance of first n natural numbers = (n²-1)/12. This is a standard formula.
405: Median: middle value when data sorted. For even n, average of two middle values.
406: Mode: most frequently occurring value in dataset.
411: Regression line: b = r×(σy/σx) where r=correlation, σ=SD.
414: Normal distribution: 68% within 1 SD, 95% within 2 SD.
419: Skewness: positive means tail on right (mean>median>mode).
421: Median for grouped data using interpolation formula.
422: Mode for grouped data = L + (f1-f0)/(2f1-f0-f2) × h.
425: Combined mean: use weighted average formula.
427: Probability with replacement: each draw independent.
428: P(second red | first red without replacement) = (r-1)/(n-1).
435: Frequency polygon: plot midpoints of class intervals.
436: Two-way table: find missing value using row/column totals.
437: Conditional probability from given two-way table data.
438: P(A and B independent) means P(A∩B)=P(A)×P(B). Verify from data.
439: Mutually exclusive: P(A∪B)=P(A)+P(B). Check if P(A∩B)=0.
440: Expected value of a function of X: E[g(X)] = Σg(xi)P(xi).
441: Probability density function: ∫f(x)dx = 1 over entire range.
442: CDF: F(x) = P(X≤x). Find probability from CDF values.
443: Chi-square test: used for testing independence of attributes.
444: T-test: used when population SD unknown and sample size small.
445: F-test / ANOVA: used to compare variances of multiple groups.
446: Type I error: rejecting true null hypothesis (false positive).
447: Type II error: accepting false null hypothesis (false negative).
448: p-value < significance level → reject null hypothesis.
449: Confidence interval: estimate ± z×(σ/√n) for given confidence %.
450: Sample size calculation: n = (z×σ/E)² where E=margin of error.
